task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D map viewer shows an aerial view of Niagara Falls (Horseshoe and American Falls).

task_completion.description: Create placemarks for Horseshoe Falls endpoints
task_completion.visual_evidence: Placemarks labeled 'Horseshoe Falls West End' and 'Horseshoe Falls East End' are visible on the map or in the Places sidebar.

task_completion.description: Create placemarks for American Falls endpoints
task_completion.visual_evidence: Placemarks labeled 'American Falls South End' and 'American Falls North End' are visible on the map or in the Places sidebar.

task_completion.description: Group placemarks in 'Niagara Falls Measurements' folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Places' sidebar displays a folder named 'Niagara Falls Measurements' containing the four specific placemarks.

task_completion.description: Add width measurements to folder description
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Properties' dialog or info balloon for the 'Niagara Falls Measurements' folder is visible, showing text that includes the width of both falls and a combined total.

task_completion.description: Save KML file to specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog or file manager confirms the file is being saved as 'niagara_falls_measurements.kml' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify agent used the Google Earth GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Google Earth application interface (map, sidebar, toolbar) rather than a terminal or script.

integrity.description: Verify KML was not manually written
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen typing raw KML/XML code into a text editor to generate the file.
The agent uses the Ruler tool or inspects map coordinates/properties to derive the width, rather than searching for the answer in a web browser.
